title: Titanium alloy products and methods of making the same
abstract: The present disclosure relates to new titanium alloys and methods for making the same. The new titanium alloys generally include 4.75 - 6.75 wt. % Al, 6.5 - 8.5 wt. % (Nb + Ta), 1.5 - 3.5 wt. % Sn, up to 5.0 wt. % Zr, and up to 2.5 wt. % Mo, the balance being titanium, optional incidental elements, and impurities. Due to their composition and methods of manufacture, the new titanium alloys may find use in several applications, such as aerospace and/or automotive applications.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A titanium alloy comprising: 4.75 - 6.75 wt. % Al; 6.5 - 8.5 wt. % (Nb + Ta); 1.5 - 3.5 wt. % Sn; up to 5.0 wt. % Zr; up to 2.5 wt. % Mo; the balance being titanium, optional incidental elements, and impurities. 2. The titanium alloy of claim 1, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 5.1 % Al. 3. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 0.5 wt. % Zr. 4. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 2.5 wt. % Zr. 5. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 0.1 wt. % Mo. 6. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 0.3 wt. % Mo. 7. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 1.75 wt. % Mo. 8. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 2.0 wt. % Sn. 9. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 2.5 wt. % Sn. 10. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes not greater than 8.25 wt. % (Nb + Ta). 11. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 6.75 wt. % (Nb + Ta). 12. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein (wt. % Nb + Ta) + (wt. % Mo) is at least 7.25. 13. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein (wt. % Sn) + (wt. % Zr) is at least 4.0. 14. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes tantalum only as an impurity. 15. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the optional incidental elements include: up to 0.6 wt. % Si; up to 0.20 wt. % O; and up to 0.10 wt. % C. 16. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 0.1 wt. % Si. 17. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes up to 0.10 wt. % O. 18. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy includes at least 0.01 wt. % C. 19. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the impurities include: up to 0.1 wt. % Fe; up to 0.1 wt. % Ni; up to 0.3 wt. % Cu; and 

 up to 0.3 wt. % Cr. 20. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy realizes a tensile yield strength of at least 90 ksi at 800Â°F, and wherein the tensile yield strength is measured in accordance with ASTM E2l-09a. 21. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy realizes an elongation of at least 8.0% at 800Â°F, and wherein the elongation when measured in accordance with ASTM E21 -09a. 22. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy realizes a creep deformation of not greater than 0.6%, wherein: the creep deformation is measured at a stress of 95 ksi and at a temperature of 800Â°F; wherein the creep deformation is measured in accordance with ASTM E139-11; and wherein the creep deformation is measured at a time of 35 hours. 23. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy realizes a creep deformation of not greater than 0.6%, wherein: the creep deformation is measured at a stress of 95 ksi and at a temperature of 800Â°F; wherein the creep deformation is measured in accordance with ASTM E139-11; and wherein the creep deformation is measured at a time of 100 hours. 24. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy realizes a room temperature dwell fatigue life of at least 2500 cycles, wherein: the room temperature dwell fatigue life is measured at a maximum fatigue stress that corresponds to 95% of a room temperature yield strength of the titanium alloy; wherein the room temperature yield strength of the alloy is measured in accordance with ASTM E8-l5a; and wherein the room temperature dwell fatigue life is measured in accordance with ASTM E466-15. 25. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy realizes a room temperature low cycle fatigue life of at least 10,000 cycles, wherein: 

 the room temperature low cycle fatigue life is measured at a maximum fatigue stress that corresponds to 95% of a room temperature yield strength of the titanium alloy; wherein the room temperature yield strength of the titanium alloy is measured in accordance with ASTM E8-l5a; and wherein the room temperature low cycle fatigue life is measured in accordance with ASTM E466-15. 26. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein a ratio of the room temperature low cycle fatigue life to the room temperature dwell fatigue life is not greater than 10; wherein both the room temperature dwell fatigue life and room temperature low cycle fatigue life are measured at a maximum fatigue stress that corresponds to 95% of a room temperature yield strength of the titanium alloy; wherein the room temperature yield strength of the titanium alloy is measured in accordance with ASTM E8-l5a; and wherein both the room temperature low cycle fatigue life and room temperature dwell fatigue life are measured in accordance with ASTM E466-15. 27. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy is in the form of a wrought product. 28. The titanium alloy of claim 27, wherein the wrought product is a rolled product, an extruded product or a forged product. 29. The titanium alloy of any of claims 1-26, wherein the titanium alloy is a shape casting. 30. The titanium alloy of any of claims 1-26, wherein the titanium alloy is an additive manufacturing feedstock. 31. The titanium alloy of claim 30, wherein the additive manufacturing feedstock is one of a powder and a wire. 32. The titanium alloy of any of claims 1-26, wherein the titanium alloy is an additively manufactured product. 33. The titanium alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the titanium alloy is an automotive component or an aerospace component.
